title: Studies on K-symbol analytic functions using modified fractional calculus
author: Aldawish, Ibrahim

Example 3. the K-symbol derivative of g(y)=1 isDkγg(y)=(1−γ)y−(k+γ−1)kkΓk(1−γ+k).=(1−γ)y−(k+γ−1)kk1−γ+kkΓ(1−γ+kk).
The K-symbol Caputo calculus is given by(1)CDkγg(y)={kwLkwk−γg(w)(y)=kw−1Γk(wk−γ)∫0yg(w)(υ)(y−υ)γ/k−w+1dυ,(w−1)k<γ<wkdwdywg(y),wk=γ.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The incentive's gamma function, also referred to as the K-symbol gamma function, is defined in the following manner [1] (see Fig. 1):Γk(u)=limm→∞m!km(mk)uk−1(u)m,k,k>0where(u)m,k≔u(u+k)(u+2k)...(u+(m−1)k)and(u)m,k=Γk(u+mk)Γk(u).Fig. 1The plot of Γ1(u),Γ2(u),Γ3(u),Γ4(u),respectively.Fig 1

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Definition 1The design of the K-symbol Riemann-Liouville fractional integral is as follows:La,kγg(y)=1kΓk(γ)∫ay(y−υ)γ/k−1g(υ)dυ.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Moreover, for γ∈(0,1) the formula for the K-Riemann-Liouville singular kernel isJkγ(υ)=(υ)γ/k−1kΓk(γ),a=0.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Note that, when g(y)=1, we haveLa,kγg(y)=(y−a)γ/kΓk(γ+k),γ∈(0,1].

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: As an exception, when a=0, we obtain (see Fig. 2)Lkγg(y)=1kΓk(γ)∫0y(y−υ)γ/k−1g(υ)dυ.Fig. 2The real and the imaginary parts of the K-symbol integral Lk0.5,Lk0.75respectively.Fig 2

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: It is clear that when k=1, we receive the normal Riemann-Liouville fractional integral propertyL1γg(y)=(Γ(α+1)Γ(α+γ+1))yα+γ.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Definition 2The K-symbol Riemann-Liouville fractional derivative is formulated by the following equality, when γ∈(0,1]:Dkγg(y)=ddy(Lk1−γg(y)).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: While, for g(y)=yα/k we get (see Fig. 3)Dkγg(y)=(α+1−γ)kγ−1−kk(Γ(α+kk)Γ(α−γ+k+1k))yα−γk.Fig. 3The real and the imaginary parts of the K-symbol integral Dk0.5,Dk0.75respectively.Fig 3

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Clearly, when k=1, we obtain the normal fractional derivativeD1γg(y)=(Γ(α+1)Γ(α−γ+1))yα−γ,γ∈[0,1].

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Keep in mind that we get the traditional Caputo derivative when k=1. Additionally, the Mittag-Leffler form function can be described in the following way dependent on the K-symbol gamma function:Ξα,βk(y)=∑m=0∞ymΓk(αm+β).2.Geometric method

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Assume that Λn is the class of analytic functions ℘ in Δ≔{ζ∈C:|ζ|<1} with ℘(m)(0)=0 for m=0,1,...,n, where ℘(0)(0)=℘(0). And that ω(0)(ζ)=ω(ζ), the set of n-dimensional Schwarz functions is defined, as follows:Sn={ω:ωisanalytic,|ω(ζ)|<1inΔandω(m)(0)=0form=0,1,...,n}.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Note that when n=0, we obtain the well-known the set of Schwarz functions. Analytic function ρ in Δ is in the normalized subclass Λ if it admits ρ(0)=ρ′(0)−1=0. This class contains all the univalent functions S⊂Λ. The class S involves two geometric functions, the convex functions in the set K, and the starlike functions in the set S*. For η(ζ) and ϑ(ζ), if an analytic function ϖ(ζ)∈Δ occurs and satisfies the conditions ϖ(0)=0,|ϖ(ζ)|<1 and η(ζ)=ϑ(ϖ(ζ) then η(ζ) is stated to be subordinate to ϑ(ζ)(see[10]).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Lemma 1(Jack Lemma) Withv(0)=0, assume thatv(ζ)is analytic inΔ. At the pointζ0∈Δ, where|v(ζ)|reaches its maximum value on the circle|ζ|=r,the derivative satisfies the equalityζ0v(ζ0)′=℘v(ζ0), where℘≥1is a real number.3.K-Symbol functional class of analytic functions

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: The collection of all functions that are analytic in a particular domain is represented by the symbolO, which stands for the functional class of analytic functions. The specific characteristics of this class are determined by the behavior of the function within the domain and by the characteristics of the domain itself. There are many different functions with smoothness and regularity qualities in the complex plane, and they are all included in the functional class of analytic functions. These functions are crucial to complicated analysis and have a wide range of uses in physics and mathematics. Functions with fractional derivatives of a specific order are within the fractional class of analytic functions. Fractional differential equations or fractional power series expansions can be used to model these functions. In this part, by using the K-symbol Riemann-Liouville fractional derivative Dkγ (similarly for CDkγ).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Proposition 2Forg∈Λ, whereΛindicates the class of normalized analytic functions inΔtaking the formg(ζ)=ζ+∑n=2∞gnζn,ζ∈Δ, thenDkγg(ζ)=(Γ(2k−γ+1k)(k+1−γ)kγ−1−kk)ζγkDkγg(ζ)is in Λ.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Proof. The K-symbol fractional derative is given, as follows:Dkγg(ζ)=Dkγ(ζ+∑n=2∞gnζn)=Dkγζk/k+∑n=2∞gnDkγζnk/k=(k+1−γ)kγ−1−kk(Γ(2kk)Γ(k−γ+k+1k))ζk−γk+∑n=2∞gn(nk+1−γ)kγ−1−kk(Γ(nk+kk)Γ(nk−γ+k+1k))ζnk−γk=(k+1−γ)kγ−1−kk(Γ(2)Γ(2k−γ+1k))ζk−γk+∑n=2∞gn(nk+1−γ)kγ−1−kk(Γ(n+1)Γ((n+1)k−γ+1k))ζnk−γk=((k+1−γ)kγ−1−kkΓ(2k−γ+1k))ζk−γk+∑n=2∞gn(nk+1−γ)kγ−1−kk(Γ(n+1)Γ((n+1)k−γ+1k))ζnk−γk.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: To normalize the above function, we define the K-symbol normalized fractional derivative, as follows:Dkγg(ζ)≔(Γ(2k−γ+1k)(k+1−γ)kγ−1−kk)ζγkDkγg(ζ).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Thus, we obtain the following K-symbol normalized fractional derivative:(2)Dkγg(ζ)≔ζ+∑n=2∞gn(Γ(2k−γ+1k)(k+1−γ)kγ−1−kk)((nk+1−γ)kγ−1−kk)(Γ(n+1)Γ((n+1)k−γ+1k))ζn≔ζ+∑n=2∞Θn(k)gnζn,whereΘn(k)≔(Γ(2k−γ+1k)(k+1−γ)kγ−1−kk)((nk+1−γ)kγ−1−kk)(Γ(n+1)Γ((n+1)k−γ+1k)).

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Obviously, D11g(ζ) presents the Salagean's differential operator [12], when Θn(1)=n. Our aim is to study the geometric properties of the operator (2) with a connection with the main value k>0.
